title: Warmly Spiced Irish Ground Pork Dumplings
description: A tangy and nostalgic Irish dish featuring ground pork, perfect for a comforting meal, this recipe is pescatarian-friendly and features a lacquer-glaze.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground pork, chopped onion, grated carrot, flour, baking powder, salt, black pepper, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
Mix the ingredients together with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Use your hands to shape the mixture into small dumplings, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Place the dumplings on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each dumpling.
Brush the tops of the dumplings with the lacquer glaze.
Drizzle the vegetable oil over the dumplings.
Bake the dumplings in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until cooked through.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
